discussion_posts.name: AshleyMeow
discussion_posts.content: Product 2, G4 is also between 2019-1-1 to 2019-3-31, why in the correct answer it is not supposed to be included? Or am I understanding the problem wrong? Thank yo uso much !

discussion_posts.name: rohitahuja823
discussion_posts.content:
Select p.product_id, p.product_name
from Product p join Sales s
on p.product_id = s.product_id
group by s.product_id
having min(s.sale_date) >= '2019-01-01' and max(sale_date) <= '2019-03-31'

discussion_posts.name: Chloe4max
discussion_posts.content:
Many people might think my solution is redundant while they only need 1 condition ''where sale_date < '2019-01-01' or sale_date > '2019-03-31'' and pass all the test cases. However I think my solution also covers the case when the products have never been sold.

select distinct
p.product_id,
p.product_name
from Product p
left join Sales s on p.product_id = s.product_id
where sale_date between '2019-01-01' and '2019-03-31'
and p.product_id not in (select distinct product_id from Sales where sale_date > '2019-03-31' or  sale_date < '2019-01-01')

discussion_posts.name: Gensun
discussion_posts.content:
# Write your MySQL query statement below
SELECT P1.product_id, P1.product_name
FROM Product AS P1
WHERE P1.product_id NOT IN
(SELECT S1.product_id
FROM Sales AS S1
WHERE S1.sale_date NOT BETWEEN '2019-01-01' AND '2019-03-31')

discussion_posts.name: nynptel
discussion_posts.content:
`select s.product_id, product_name
from Sales s
left join Product p on s.product_id = p.product_id
group by product_id
having min(sale_date) >= '2019-01-01' and max(sale_date) <= '2019-03-31'`

discussion_posts.name: Sachi12345
discussion_posts.content:
# Write your MySQL query statement below


SELECT product_id,product_NAME FROM Product 
WHERE product_id IN (
SELECT product_id FROM Sales GROUP BY 1 HAVING
MAX(sale_date) <= CAST('2019-03-31' AS DATE)
AND MIN(sale_date) >= CAST('2019-01-01' AS DATE))
